transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
And I'm on mute.
Good morning, all.
How's it going?
Good morning.
Eric, I don't even know if we've been on a call together yet, because I think I've been out since you started joining the calls, but I'm Jessalynn.
Yeah, nice to meet you, Jessalynn.
I'm Eric.
Pleasure.
I'm the managing editor on y'all's account, so I work a lot with the content and then also some of the strategy stuff, too.
So, yeah, it's nice to have another set of eyes on the content and moving things along and stuff, too.
So excited to have you here.
I'm trying to remember, what is your role at WebStacks?
So I, digital marketing, I do, or lead, I should say, all of our internal marketing initiatives.
Okay.
I'm slowly getting merged into some of the...
The that y'all been doing for the last, I think it's been a year, right?
Or a little over a year.
Yeah.
So yeah, I started joining these a couple of weeks ago.
But yeah, super excited to continue to collaborate.
Nice, nice.
Well.
I had a quick update, actually.
So Jesse won't be joining the call, so you guys just have me today.
I think he's not feeling too well, so I just wanted to provide that update.
Oh, OK.
Well, hopefully he starts feeling better soon.
I know we're entering that time of year where people are just dropping like flies.
So I'm sure it'll be one of us next week.
But we can go ahead and start digging into some agenda items.
We don't have a whole lot to go over, but we do have a couple of things.
We can follow up with Jesse on the Jason export when he's feeling better, maybe.
But I think we're still.
Yeah.
Thank on that one.
I actually, I didn't get a chance to touch base with you on this yet, Rachel, but do we have any updates on the coffee chat series graphics?
No.
I'm sorry, I was thinking of the other one that you were managing.
So the coffee chat series graphics was with your team, Eric.
Do we have any updates on that?
Yeah, I can give a brief update actually on that as well as the articles that have been ready for review for the last few days.
So those three articles are good to go.
I spoke with Jesse earlier this morning, so we can get those going.
And I know that the holdup for the graphics, I have provided an update last week that we're getting some sort of branded repeatable templates that we can use for these articles.
Those are good to go.
One question that I had was, how should I be sharing these graphics with the GrowthX team?
Is there an existing Google Drive?
Is
That I can create a new folder in, I'm not too familiar with how the graphic creation or, I guess, like handoff is with the team here.
Yeah, so typically for most of the blogs, we have, like, an image generator as part of our pipeline, and we just go ahead and generate those for y'all.
And we link them actually in Airtable.
So you can either link them in our Airtable base.
There's a column for it, which I can show you here, or if it's easier for you, can also just dump them in our, in our shared drive, whichever works best for you.
Let me, again, send you a link to both of those.
What would be your preference?
Like, what's easier for you to either just drop them in our shared drive or to link them in our Airtable base?
I can link them in the Airtable if that's easiest.
I don't mind.
That's perfectly fine.
I believe I have access to it.
So.
Yeah, can, let me share my other screen here and I can pull it up for you so you can see, typically you can, let's see, there's a column in our Airtable base where you can just link it directly.
Mm-hmm.
You just added the file, right?
I'm looking at it right here.
Yeah.
Gotcha.
And we can generate alt text for them and all of that, but yeah, if you just can drop it right in here, that would be great.
Cool.
I can add those, um, after the call and yeah, those should be good to go.
Awesome.
And yeah, I believe that's it for that.
that's, um, so, Jeff, um, that's Oh So, uh,
Yeah, that's the update for those points there.
Okay, cool.
Perfect.
And then the other thing that we wanted to walk through today is just kind of the workflow that Jesse requested us kind of flesh out a little bit for the coffee chat series.
So I think Jacob put together some stuff for us to go over.
Do you want to share your screen, Jacob?
I'll just chat that one verbally because I kind of pivoted a little bit there.
So the update for this week was that I ran through a chat, one of those coffee chat, the enterprise website, and we picked some new topics based off of that.
And then there were a decent number.
So this is where I kind of wanted to pause.
I was finding some old content that was good for internal linking, adding sections to highlight those coffee chats and found a decent number of those right away.
And then just looking at like GSC for impressions.

Graphic, et cetera, those were, a bunch of those were good candidates for refresh, which we do want to do, and I think it's like been kind of in our chats over the last couple of months, there's so much content that you all have done, stuff before us, stuff that even in our early days that now needs refreshing, and so, but I want to do that, like, we've identified a few of those to add sections for, so we're, I believe, going forward with those, but at the larger scale, I want to do a bigger audit and analysis, and like, we're doing this ad hoc right now in small doses, and it's just not scalable, so I want to get those, that export from you all on the, all the blogs with categories and the tags, and that will help me to get a better sense, get a whole lay of the land, and then we can, instead of going like little cluster and little article by little article and doing a couple at a time and not keeping track of that as a bigger project, I want to roll that in, so that's just been like something that each of these like little things that we've done keeps highlighting to me the importance of doing that sooner than later, so much.

Wanted to discuss that with you all, but I think that's a logical next sense, next step, and then, so that's one piece, so in the short term, it's, we picked some additional articles that confirmed that we didn't have any content around those, they should connect to these coffee chats, and they fit within clusters that we've already done, so we're working on those on top of the other articles that we're doing this week were previously approved.
Going forward, I do want to build that process, we just had enough content to get through this week, and I think next, and so I didn't go forward and build that whole process.

The other thing I did in lieu of that, so kind of like outline, I can share my screen for this, there's like two pieces in here, sorry, I'm kind of trying to hit a lot of things at once, but I built like a project plan for an audit, potentially a prune if we choose to do that, maybe a matter of just identifying articles for refresh, maybe could be, identify

Identifying articles to condense, consolidate, even potentially prune if we have cannibalization, so I built that into a bunch of tasks here, largely that's on me, but I did put you on one thing, like adding tags as we figure out what's necessary here, and I've tagged Jesse on exporting that list, but if that comes from anyone on your team, it doesn't matter to me, I just started with Jesse, and then, so there's a bunch of tasks, those will happen over the next couple weeks, depending that we're all aligned here.

And then, what I started to do, this is not fully built out, but spent a decent chunk of this morning trying to identify the different projects that we have, we have just like a decent number of balls in the air, and we've had conversations over the past few months on things we can do, and so I just want to build out a project board for all of us, so have more visibility and more, just don't let things drop, so it's like, you need build out all the steps for coffee chats and where we are, created a project board,
For like our data cleanup, this is partly that tags in categories export that will trickle into connecting those categories to our air table.
So looker will reflect what you have on the backend.
I found this like, I'm starting to find little things that we should fix.
This is what I tagged, where'd it go?
Oh, this is what I tagged you and Eric that, I noticed things like if I select Gatsby or any of our technologies, there's no articles.
Well, I guess I found one article here, but generally speaking, these aren't being used.
Our industries don't seem to be, there's just some cleanup things that I want.
I don't think I've found all the issues yet, but as I'm doing some of these diggings in, I don't want to lose these pieces.
So I'm starting to connect them here into larger projects.
And then the last piece is like anything that we've talked about historically, I'm trying to go back through our Fathoms and old agendas.

just haven't gotten to all of those yet, but for instance, like Jesse and I outlined a programmatic project that we started on and it got held up by Dev Resources on your end, I believe, so I'm like, I'm just trying to get everything into one space so we can see that both, you know, 30,000 foot view and then the individual tasks that we are working on right now that are beyond, you know, the day-to-day publishing.
I don't think that that needs to be in here, but I just want to create this, get y'all, everyone on this call's eyes.
think people are just seeing this for the first time.

There's a lot of this morning, but what, you know, I would like us to, if everyone's on board, start using this, identify where it's falling flat, what connections we can make to other things, just make this as useful as possible so we can get as much done.
Because again, we, we do a lot of things and want to just keep them all in order and have eyesight into where they all are.
So work in progress here.
progress.
This is what I've been spending my time on, on top of, like, quickly making sure we had enough on the coffee chats for this week, but want to build that out into something more systemic.
Systematic is probably the word.
Cool.
Yeah, this looks good.
I will, in terms of high priority items, I'll make sure that we give you that export as quickly as possible with the categories and tags.
Yeah, for this one issue here regarding the categorization on our blog, I think I would have to look with Insanity to see if there's a workaround and maybe we're just not adding those tags, perhaps.
Could also be like a dev issue, so hopefully it's not a dev issue.
And it's just the tagging that I just mentioned, but I can look into this as well.
In terms of high priority items in addition to those, are there any others that
I should account for or get Jesse's eyes on immediately.
The export is the number one thing right now.
I think there are going to be other things.
I'm just trying to go through previous agendas and get anything in here that hasn't been wrapped up.
And then as we dig into anything else, like new coffee chat process, there will be next steps and things that come out.
I just want to, I think it's like we have an agenda, I think that wasn't connected enough.
And I know Jesse talked with like Marcel and Andy, and that was some of the pieces, like, let's have all this documentation.
like, yeah, some of that exists, but it doesn't all.
So I was like, well, for both sides, like that's good.
For you, the immediate next step is this blog export.
A few things have dependencies on that, and that will allow me to start like a prune process.
It will let us do some of the cleanup on data, making our looker more useful for you all.
Like, there's just a bunch of things that this really will help us, you know, that thing that you're calling out, like these tags and things, all these

So that was just a first step, so do this one, maybe it doesn't matter, mark this more of a priority, and then by next call, we should have some additional next steps for both sides, and with Jessi, hopefully, vacuum feeling better, and this more built out, we can actually, like, come into the call, have reviewed this, and say, like, here are our priorities, we can balance what this is, start to build, like, timelines for these, we can look at this with a calendar view, and have a better sense of, like, what are we doing?

this quarter, what's actually manageable, what are, because I think we, this whole group comes up with a lot of things, again, like, what is the order, how do we get these all done, make sure we get everything we want to, so, um, do this, and then by next week, I think, I'll send a slack when I feel like this is more robust and thorough, um, so we can review it ahead of next call, so that would be the other takeaway by next call.
Awesome, sounds good.
Sorry, I know there's a lot, I feel like I'm caffeinated right now, and

I'm just chatting a mile a minute, so let me know if there's any questions, since again, this is V1, I'm like not tied to how exactly I did this, or if there's any other fields, connectors, whatever, please just let's make this as useful as possible for everybody.
Yeah, this looks good.
Yeah, I mean, I'll poke around and see if there's any improvements, but like just at first glance, it looks, it looks good.
Cool, it'll be a living, living project manager, I'll send that over to the Slack as well.
Yeah, thanks for bringing that together.
think that'll be super helpful.

Like you said, Jakub, I feel like I love our calls with y'all because we come up with so many great ideas, but I think this will really help like galvanize our efforts a little bit more just to keep us all on the same page with priorities and progress and all that.
So, yeah, I think that's all that we had for today, unless there's anything else that you need from us, Eric.
I just thought of a question actually since we were looking at the board.

In a case where, maybe that's something that we can figure out or is still in progress, but in the case of I, you know, conduct a couple coffee chats in a week because realistically I think the floor here is maybe one per week.
Max is probably two to three.

I just had two this week, for instance, and maybe again, Jacob, you're probably still figuring what that would look like, but like once I can have those coffee chats, would I be the one ideally to go into the board and add it or what would that communication look like?
Because previously I think I was just sharing the links in the, in the Slack.
So yeah, what does that look like ideally?
Yeah, that is not fully figured out on my end.
so, and would be a conversation.
It could be something that you create and then add, like we create a template for the coffee chats.
That's like once we.

Oh, the coffee chat process, I think that would be necessary to do this, but in that instance, say we had built that out, you can build a template here, so then you could come in like new coffee chat, it has all the fields populated, and tag Jessalynn or Rachel, again, depending on what we've defined as that process, and then it auto-populates with like what we do for each coffee chat, so I would imagine that that's the process, Jessalynn, you know, does that fit in your workflows, or would we want to go?

Yeah, my only hesitation with that is just like I think the priority for the board was to be like high level task management of like things outside of like content production, and most of our content production currently lives in Airtable, so I just wouldn't want to create duplicate work where we're creating like a card in Notion, and then also creating a card in Airtable, so my instinct is actually that we would just create a new article I don't want thing.
So in Airtable and drop the link to the coffee chat there.
That's kind of my instinct, but do you feel comfortable in Airtable, Eric, or do you have a preference like what would work best with your workflow?

Because we can really tailor it to you, but my instinct is to just keep content in Airtable, like content production, and then just keep the high-level tasks and things outside of the actual individual content productions in Notion for those kind of high-level priorities.
Yeah, mean, Airtable is not a, not a, I'm familiar with Airtable is what I'm trying to say, but yeah, that works with me.
I was just curious and also don't want to complicate things and like you said, have to duplicate the work, like no one likes to do.
Yeah, I appreciate you asking the question.
So yeah, you can either drop it, think, directly in Airtable, we'll just create a new article card, tag it with coffee chats as like the, I think if we have it as topic.
Yeah.

Cluster right now, and then drop a link to the recording, either in the comments or in the article description field, I think would work great, and yeah, otherwise, you can always just drop it in Slack, and Rachel and I can add it in if that's easier for you.
No worries.
That sounds good.
Okay.
Sounds great.
Does that work for you, Rachel?
I guess I should probably ask you, too, since you're on.
Okay.
Yeah.
Yeah, sounds good.
All right.
Awesome.

Yeah, and like Jakub said, think, like, next week, once we have, like, the task board fleshed out a little bit, we can kind of reevaluate then if we want, but for now, if you can just either drop it in Slack or drop it directly in Airtable, I think that would be great.
Awesome.
That sounds good.
But yeah, that was it.
I will make sure to get those things over to you, the graphics, get those into Airtable, and then, Jakub, I'll make sure to get that export over to you ASAP.
That's good.

And if there's anything else that we were waiting on or like it comes to mind that we would want to do, feel free to ping, put in the channel or ping me directly and we can start adding to this project board because again, as I'm starting to populate anything that anyone thinks of that's like, we should have done this or we plan to do this and never did like, let's get the everything on the board and we can go from there.
Cool.
All right.
Sounds good.
Have a great day y'all.
Thanks y'all.
See you guys.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content updates and introduce a new project management board.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=308.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**New Project Board:** A Notion board was introduced to centralize all non-publishing projects (e.g., content audit, data cleanup), providing a single source of truth for tracking progress and priorities.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [**Content Audit Blocked:** A full content audit is blocked pending a blog export with categories/tags. The audit is needed to move from ad-hoc refreshes to a scalable strategy for pruning, consolidating, and updating content.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
  - [**Coffee Chat Workflow:** The process for new coffee chats will remain in Airtable to avoid duplicating work. Eric will add recordings directly or via Slack, and Jakub will build a process template on the new board.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
  - [**Immediate Deliverables:** Eric will upload 3 new graphics to Airtable and provide the full blog export to unblock the content audit.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)

## Topics

### New Project Management Board

  - [**Problem:** Ad-hoc project management and siloed communication are inefficient.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [**Solution:** Jakub created a Notion board to centralize all non-publishing initiatives.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [**Goal:** Provide a single source of truth for tracking progress, priorities, and timelines.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
  - [**Initial Projects:**](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=510.0)
      - [Content Audit & Prune](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=454.0)
      - [Data Cleanup (e.g., fixing broken blog categories like "Gatsby")](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)
      - [Programmatic SEO](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
      - [Coffee Chat Process Definition](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)

### Content Audit & Data Cleanup

  - [**Trigger:** Identifying refresh candidates during the coffee chat series highlighted the need for a scalable audit.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=327.0)
  - [**Blocker:** A full audit is blocked pending a blog export with categories and tags.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The export is required to analyze the entire content library and build a scalable strategy for pruning, consolidating, and refreshing articles.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
  - [**Related Issue:** Jakub found that blog categories (e.g., "Gatsby") are broken, showing no articles. This data cleanup task is now on the new board.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=540.0)

### Coffee Chat Series Workflow

  - [**Graphics:** New branded templates are ready. Eric will upload them directly to the Airtable `Image` column.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=123.0)
  - [**Recordings:** The workflow for new recordings will remain in Airtable to prevent duplicating content production tasks in two systems.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)
      - [**Process:** Eric adds a new article card → tags it "coffee chats" → links the recording in the description.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=1070.0)
      - [**Alternative:** Eric can post the link in Slack for Rachel/Jessalynn to add.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=1070.0)
  - [**Process Template:** Jakub will build a formal coffee chat process template on the new Notion board to standardize the workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=900.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Eric:**](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
      - [Upload 3 new graphics to the Airtable `Image` column.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=123.0)
      - [Provide the full blog export with categories and tags to Jakub.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [Investigate the broken blog category issue (e.g., "Gatsby").](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=685.0)
  - [**Jakub:**](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
      - [Flesh out the new Notion project board with more tasks and details.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)
      - [Share the completed board in Slack for review before the next meeting.](https://fathom.video/share/s3k5smbnNncAYGWTMAgcoe4TUhC1poKx?tab=summary&timestamp=810.0)

action_items.description: Add graphics for 3 ready articles to Airtable
Send blog export (categories/tags) to Jakub; then Jakub link categories to Airtable
Investigate blog categorization/tagging issue w/ Insanity; report to Jakub
Share Notion project board in Slack for review before next call
Add Coffee Chat recordings to Airtable (topic=Coffee Chats) or Slack for Jessalynn/Rachel
